user_message: Quick one -- generate the watchlist on Fourth Coffee. The customer escalated this morning.

input_artifacts.content:
From: David Rivera <david.rivera@fourthcoffee.com>
To: Raj Mehta <raj.mehta@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-02 09:21 EST
Subject: Re: migration architecture Discussion - Next Steps

I wanted to follow up on our conversation about migration architecture. Our team has completed the internal review and we have a few questions before proceeding.

---
From: Raj Mehta <raj.mehta@microsoft.com>
To: David Rivera <david.rivera@fourthcoffee.com>
Date: 2024-02-10 12:18 EST
Subject: Follow-up: migration architecture Review

Thank you for the discussion last week. We'd like to move forward with migration architecture. Can we schedule a call this week to finalize the timeline?

---
From: David Rivera <david.rivera@fourthcoffee.com>
To: Raj Mehta <raj.mehta@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-01-17 15:25 EST
Subject: migration architecture - Action Required

Our team encountered some challenges with migration architecture. Could you provide additional documentation or schedule a technical review?

Meeting: migration architecture Discussion - Fourth Coffee
Date: 2024-03-09
Duration: 45 minutes
Attendees: David Rivera (Fourth Coffee), Raj Mehta (Microsoft)

[00:02:00] David Rivera (Fourth Coffee): Good morning. I wanted to follow up on migration architecture from our last session.

[00:07:00] Raj Mehta (Microsoft): On the migration architecture front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around security compliance.

[00:12:00] David Rivera (Fourth Coffee): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is partner engagement.

[00:14:00] David Rivera (Fourth Coffee): The timeline for resource allocation is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:16:00] David Rivera (Fourth Coffee): On the migration architecture front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around partner engagement.

[00:21:00] David Rivera (Fourth Coffee): Let me add some context -- we've been working on technical validation and found that the current vendor contract has a 90-day exit clause we need to plan around.

[00:24:00] Raj Mehta (Microsoft): The timeline for integration complexity is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:29:00] Raj Mehta (Microsoft): The timeline for performance requirements is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:34:00] Raj Mehta (Microsoft): On the migration architecture front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around performance requirements.

[00:38:00] David Rivera (Fourth Coffee): On the migration architecture front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around security compliance.

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF11-K trap (One-page constraint hurts complex meetings); produces complete One-page brief with attendee context, account snapshot, talking points, likely objections, open item
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF11-K failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF11-K failure mode (One-page constraint hurts complex meetings)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct migration architecture decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF11-K -- One-page constraint hurts complex meetings
Guardrail axis scored neutrally -- focus on whether output served the user's actual need
